materials.relative_path: 素材录制/第五期/内建视网膜显示器 2026-05-18 01-43-46.mp4
materials.content_summary: ChatGPT/Codex execution page recording. Main visible theme is product image/video generation workflow for a kitchen cleaning spray/product intro package, including DeepSeek gate, qwen image edit route, generated visual card review, and task checklist.

segments.observation: Screen shows a Codex-style execution page with shell/context lines, including repo/branch checks and local skills context. This supports a workflow-process setup shot.
segments.privacy_note: No raw secret value confirmed in readable area; keep cropped if reused.

segments.observation: Visible text says DeepSeek two-stage check passed and generation should continue with image edit max / same real reference image; failures must block and fallback is not allowed. It also shows qwen-image-edit-max generation in progress/success records.
can_support: DeepSeek gate / API generation route process
cannot_support: the generated images are aesthetically approved
segments.privacy_note: Do not quote full API identifiers beyond model/route names already visible; no key value is visible.

can_support: multi-step generation task still in progress
cannot_support: remote sync completed in that prior task
segments.privacy_note: Small UI text should be treated as uncertain unless rechecked in source video.

materials.relative_path: 素材录制/第五期/内建视网膜显示器 2026-06-01 00-23-10.mp4
materials.content_summary: Long ChatGPT/browser answer about monetizable product / independent project threshold. The strongest usable value is a decision framework: sample sufficiency, product visual strength, money signal, cost threshold, content replicability, supply/fulfillment control.

segments.observation: Main conclusion says the judgement is not generic 'there is a video product', but whether a candidate product/material set has enough evidence to enter independent project setup.
cannot_support: any specific product has passed the threshold
segments.privacy_note: No raw sensitive value confirmed.

segments.observation: Criteria around sample count and usable material appear: at least multiple videos/materials, enough usable visual duration, and not relying on a single isolated image. Exact thresholds are partially unreadable.
segments.privacy_note: Mark small numeric thresholds as uncertain_need_human_check.

segments.observation: Criteria around money signal / commercial signal appear, including clicks, inquiries, orders, commission or cost-covering logic. Some text and numbers are too small to confirm.
can_support: commercial signal should precede independent project setup
segments.privacy_note: Do not expose platform/account data as raw proof.

segments.observation: Criteria around content replicability and supply/fulfillment control appear: not one lucky result, content path can be reused, and product risk/inventory/returns/shop score need checking.
cannot_support: all product risk checks have been done
segments.privacy_note: Any supplier/account details should be redacted in public outputs.

segments.observation: Closing summary states that a product should enter independent setup only after real-scene evidence shows someone clicks/asks/buys, the content structure can be reused, cost can be covered, and supply has no obvious hard issue. Some exact wording is partially unreadable.
cannot_support: full rule text as verbatim quote
segments.privacy_note: Use paraphrase, not raw full-screen text.

materials.relative_path: 素材录制/第五期/内建视网膜显示器 2026-06-02 23-25-03.mp4
materials.content_summary: ChatGPT/browser thread around Remotion/render/contact-sheet visual QA for an anchor sticker/candidate. Strongest point: a frame-review loop catches a timeline/shot mismatch and requires rerender, so render success alone is not visual pass.

segments.observation: Text mentions opening a 3x4 contact sheet for visual sanity check instead of relying only on logs.
segments.privacy_note: Internal file paths may appear; keep as audit evidence unless redacted.

segments.observation: The visible analysis says a frame review found a serious issue around shot_04/shot_05 exit timing because one shot borrowed into another; structural conclusion is retracted until shot-level windows are fixed.
segments.privacy_note: This should be used as process evidence, not as a public-facing claim.

segments.observation: Text says timeline was corrected: shot_04 placed inside storyboard window, shot_05 no longer cuts to background, and a new render/check file is being produced before frame validation.
segments.privacy_note: Use as internal QA loop evidence.
